Fan transparency · Live events app
Ticket Opportunity Intelligence™
OnTour is a fan-facing app that makes the most competitive ticket onsales in the world understandable — before fans ever enter the queue.
Fans experience superstar onsales as chaos, opacity and failure.
In reality, these events behave more like the Super Bowl or Formula 1 than a normal retail transaction.
Massive global demand meets fixed supply.
The outcomes feel extreme because they are extreme.
The problem is not demand.
The problem is understanding demand.

onTour publishes at announcement — before the on-sale, before the queue rage, before the Senate subpoena.
About onTour
onTour is a fan-facing intelligence layer that makes high-demand ticketing environments understandable.
It does not change how tickets are priced, allocated, or sold.
It makes the system visible.
Context
Superstar tours now operate at global scale, where demand can exceed available tickets by multiples.
Outcomes are shaped by timing, access tiers, geography, and release patterns — not just demand alone.
To fans, these dynamics are largely invisible.
This gap drives confusion, frustration, and distrust.
How it works
Tour Page — goes live at the moment a tour is announced and leading into onsale, communicating scale, markets, expected demand, and relevant historical tour data.
Previous tour performance — including sell-outs, added shows, and demand patterns — provides immediate context for new announcements.
Historical data may include primary and secondary ticket pricing, grosses, and demand-to-supply signals where available.
Your Chances — frames demand relative to supply and outlines realistic paths to access.
Track Record — surfaces historical tour performance to contextualize new announcements.
Inside the Tour — provides context on production scale, routing, and operational complexity.
My Tours — allows fans to track demand shifts, added shows, and emerging ticket opportunities in real time.
What it does
Sets expectations before onsale.
Reduces perceived randomness in outcomes.
Provides a consistent, neutral reference point for fans.
Historical context helps frame new announcements against familiar patterns, making expectations clearer from the outset.
Helps align perception with reality.
What it enables now
Deploys alongside tour announcements as a fan-facing reference layer.
A scalable framework for communicating demand, access, availability, and historical context.
Positioned to integrate naturally into tour rollout and communication flows.
A way to improve transparency without altering commercial models.
Core insight
The problem is not demand.
The problem is understanding demand.
In a system defined by high demand and limited supply, transparency reshapes the experience.
The Live Nation cooperation case
Live Nation posted $25.2B in 2025 revenue, hosted 159M fans, and has already booked 80%+ of its 2026 large-venue calendar. It also faces a monopoly verdict, FTC suit, DOJ settlement, $280M payout, and an internal executive caught on record boasting about "robbing fans blind." In that context, a tool that publishes honest supply/demand math at every announcement changes the narrative from "price-gouging monopolist" to "the company that finally tells fans the truth." That is worth more than any legal settlement.
Sources — Taylor Swift / Eras Tour
$2,077,618,725 gross · 10,168,008 tickets · 149 shows: Billboard Boxscore Dec 2024. NYT, Variety confirmed. Guinness World Records certified.
Avg face value $204: Billboard Boxscore / Pollstar / Guinness ($204.33).
Resale: $1,652 avg (tour) · $2,952 (Vancouver) · $3,801 (N. America '23): Victory Live / Business Insider / Variety Dec 2024.
3.5M Verified Fan registrants · crash · 2.4M single-day record: Ticketmaster Senate testimony Jan 2023.
Senate hearing "That's the Ticket" Jan 24 2023: Senate.gov · ABC News.
$197M crew bonuses: People magazine Dec 2024.
DOJ suit May 2024 · FTC suit Sep 2025 · 5 brokers / 6,345 accounts / 246,407 tickets: DOJ press release · FTC complaint Sep 2025.
Sources — Harry Styles
$617.3M gross · 5,023,682 tickets · 169 shows: Billboard Boxscore Aug 2023.
11.5M MSG presale registrants: Ticketmaster Jan 2026. Together, Together MSG presale data.
Together, Together — 67 shows · 7 cities · 30-night MSG residency: hstyles.co.uk Jan 2026. Variety, Billboard, Wikipedia.
Avg ticket $131 (2021) → $204 (2022–23): Billboard Boxscore per-leg averages.
Production: PRG, All Access, Kinesys, Silent Partners, Fly By Nite: PLSN production feature 2018–2022.
Industry / regulatory sources
$9B+ 2023 global gross: Pollstar year-end 2023.
Live Nation $25.2B 2025 revenue (+9% YoY) · 159M fans · $20.9B concert division: Live Nation Entertainment full-year 2025 earnings release, February 2026. SEC Form 8-K.
LN: 80%+ of 2026 large-venue shows booked · concerts GTV $26B: Live Nation Q4 2025 earnings release, SEC filing.
LN: 75% of US tickets under $100 · $40 avg get-in · $60 avg stadium get-in: Live Nation Q1 2025 earnings release, SEC Form 8-K.
LN ~80% US primary ticketing at major concert venues / amphitheaters: American Economic Liberties Project policy brief June 2023 citing Pollstar data; DOJ/state AG complaint 2024 framing. Live Events Ticketing Industry statistics (WifiTalents, Feb 2026): "Ticketmaster controls an estimated 70% share of the primary ticketing market for major US venues."
LN's own defense: ~44% when measured broadly across all stadiums/arenas including sports: NBC News reporting on LN defense arguments at antitrust trial, April 2026.
TM sole provider for 82% of US amphitheaters: American Economic Liberties Project, citing Pollstar December 2022 data.
Concert division: $20.9B revenue · $687M AOI · 3.3% margin (2025): Live Nation FY2025 earnings release Feb 2026; Billboard, Art Threat, TicketNews analysis.
Ticketmaster: $3.1B revenue · $1.1B AOI · ~37% margin (2025): Live Nation FY2025 earnings release; TicketNews Feb 2026.
Sponsorship margin 64% (2025): Music Business Worldwide reporting on LN FY2025 earnings.
CTS Eventim: €3.08B 2025 revenue · 177.9M tickets · dominant in Europe: CTS Eventim FY2025 results.
LN global: 50% concert promotion market share estimate: Intel Market Research / industry estimates 2025. Note: not verified in LN's own filings — treat as industry estimate only.
LN global Ticketmaster: 346M fee-bearing tickets sold globally 2025 (per SEC): Live Nation FY2025 Q4 SEC filing.
FTC Junk Fees Rule Dec 2024 · effective May 2025: FTC.gov.
TM all-in pricing implemented 2025: Ticketmaster announcement May 2025; Wikipedia; NPR/KPBS April 2026.
DOJ settlement: 15% fee cap, $280M fund, divest 13 amphitheater agreements: CNN, NPR reporting April 2026.
Federal jury verdict April 15, 2026 · $1.72/ticket overcharge in 22 states · 33 states + DC: CNN, NPR, CBC, NBC News, April 15–16, 2026.
LN exec internal messages "robbing them blind": CBC News, NPR coverage of Live Nation antitrust trial, April 2026.
$16.4B mandatory TM fees 2019–24 · fees up to 44%: FTC complaint September 2025, reported by Reuters and ThePricer.org.
TM fees avg ~27–28% of face: The Hustle survey of 40 tickets; Ticketmaster Fee Calculator cross-reference.
GAO: avg service fee 20% · total to 50%: US Government Accountability Office report cited in Senate testimony 2023.
StubHub IPO $9.3B valuation · Vivid Seats $3.9B GOV 2024: Industry reporting 2025.
SI Tickets: $2.5B inventory · 50M tickets · 250K events · 60M monthly users: Sports Illustrated Tickets / F6S / LA28 press release March 2026.
LA28 Olympics: SI Tickets + Ticketmaster as verified resale platforms: LA28 press release March 30, 2026.
FIFA WC 2026: 500M+ ballot requests · 15% buyer+seller fee · Final listed $143,750–$230,000 on FIFA platform · $959K semi-final listing: Goal.com, Flashscore, Eurovision News Spotlight, ESPN, TrackaLacker, 2025–2026.
FIFA dynamic pricing, prices raised after 1 day of sales: Eurovision News Spotlight analysis November 2025.
NFL secondary ticket prices +173% (inflation-adj) 2015–2025 · Super Bowl peak $35,165: Axios Kansas City, February 2026, citing Gametime data.
NBA Finals avg secondary $4,150 in 2024 · Knicks avg $213: Statista / Sportscasting / BarrysTickets 2024–2025.
UK: 1 in 5 tickets on secondary · £145M extra fees/year: UK industry research late 2024.
Coldplay Mumbai: 13M fans · 90K tickets · 144:1: Consumer Choice Center / Sumsub 2024.
